lastReleaseMessage: v0.10.0: bar-raise drain (D-006 loopback bind, D-007 import/rollback strict backup-fail, error_log + logEvent, daily prune, regex hoist, analytics surface modules, dedup guard, dashboard preservation, drift-fix F1-F8); .finances/ removed from git index (D-001/D-002 enforcement)

barRaise.topFinding: The code is healthy and the doc layer is aligned for a seventh straight canonical audit, but the delivery loop around YaB is stalled for a fourth consecutive bar-raise. The repo head is still e6cac08 (2026-05-29), seven days cold; the working tree holds the F1-F11 doc batch (three modified files) plus fifteen untracked audit and bar-raise reports, and this report makes sixteen. The two structural HIGHs both carry unchanged: the uncommitted pile (maintainability) and the seven uninstalled CLAUDE.md skills that leave the audit-to-fix loop with no executor (skill-suite health). Zero of five open actions closed, and the pile grew overnight as CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-06-05.md landed and was never committed. This is not a code problem. One doc-only commit plus an executor with a commit step drains the entire backlog. Bank statements in shipped git history (MEDIUM), a stale dashboard audit block (MEDIUM), and a hand-rolled v0.10.0 that left the milestone block stale (MEDIUM) all ride alongside, still unactioned.
